context: Tests whether the agent correctly routes different argument patterns when interacting with Shortcut — including Shortcut app URLs, bare numeric IDs, and the 'my' shortcut — using the CLI instead of WebFetch and following the correct routing priority.

checklist.name: Prerequisite check command
checklist.description: Runs `which short && short --version` (or equivalent two-command check) before issuing any operational command

checklist.name: Story URL extraction
checklist.description: For a URL like `app.shortcut.com/org/story/12345`, extracts the numeric ID and calls `short story 12345` rather than using WebFetch or curl on the URL

checklist.name: Epic URL extraction
checklist.description: For a URL like `app.shortcut.com/org/epic/67890`, extracts the numeric ID and calls `short epic view 67890` rather than using WebFetch or curl on the URL

checklist.name: No WebFetch on Shortcut URLs
checklist.description: Does NOT use WebFetch, curl, or browser-fetch calls on any app.shortcut.com URL

checklist.name: Numeric ID routing
checklist.description: A bare numeric argument (e.g. `42`) is treated as a story ID and passed to `short story 42`

checklist.name: My stories routing
checklist.description: The argument `my` is routed to `short search -o me`

checklist.name: Story details fields
checklist.description: Story/epic output includes all of: ID, title, state, owner, description, and URL

checklist.name: Follow-up suggestions
checklist.description: After presenting results, the response includes at least one relevant follow-up action suggestion

checklist.name: List output routing
checklist.description: The `list` argument is routed to `short search` with no filters

checklist.name: Search text routing
checklist.description: A `search <text>` argument is routed to `short search -t "<text>"`

checklist.name: Correct CLI tool used
checklist.description: All Shortcut data retrieval uses the `short` CLI, not the Shortcut REST API directly via curl/fetch without the CLI
